SEARCH
Package edu.yu.cs.com3810.messagebus

Class MessageBus

java.lang.Object
edu.yu.cs.com3810.messagebus.MessageBus
public class MessageBus extends Object
The Message Bus — a standalone process that routes messages between all Smart Home components over persistent Netty TCP connections.

Since the bus is its own separate process, subscribers are not local lambda callbacks. Instead, the bus tracks two things:

  1. Channel registry — maps each connected node ID to its Netty Channel. Populated when a component connects and sends a REGISTER frame. Cleared when the channel goes inactive.
  2. Subscription registry — maps each TopicType to the set of node IDs currently subscribed to it. When a message arrives for a topic, the bus looks up the subscriber set, resolves each node ID to a channel, and enqueues an outbound write for each.

Inbound messages from connected components arrive via enqueueInbound(Message) called by the Netty server handler, and are placed on inboundQueue. The MessageDispatcherRunner drains this queue and calls publish(Message, String) to fan out to subscribers.

All public methods are thread-safe via ConcurrentHashMap and Collections.synchronizedSet(java.util.Set<T>).

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    MessageBus()
    Initialises the bus with empty registries and pre-creates all required topics.

    Description

  • Constructor Details

    • MessageBus

      public MessageBus()
      Initialises the bus with empty registries and pre-creates all required topics.

  • Method Details

    • registerChannel

      public void registerChannel(String nodeId, io.netty.channel.Channel channel)
      Registers a newly connected component's channel. Called by MessageBusServerHandler after a REGISTER control frame is received identifying the node.
      Parameters:
      nodeId - the ID the component identified itself with
      channel - the active Netty channel for that component

    • deregisterChannel

      public void deregisterChannel(String nodeId)
      Removes a disconnected component's channel and all its topic subscriptions. Called by MessageBusServerHandler.channelInactive(io.netty.channel.ChannelHandlerContext).
      Parameters:
      nodeId - the ID of the component that disconnected

    • subscribe

      public void subscribe(String nodeId, TopicType topic)
      Subscribes a node to a topic. From this point on, any message published to that topic will be written to the node's channel.
      Parameters:
      nodeId - the ID of the subscribing component
      topic - the topic to subscribe to
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the topic does not exist

    • unsubscribe

      public void unsubscribe(String nodeId, TopicType topic)
      Unsubscribes a node from a topic.
      Parameters:
      nodeId - the ID of the component to unsubscribe
      topic - the topic to unsubscribe from

    • enqueueInbound

      public void enqueueInbound(Message<?> message)
      Places an inbound message onto the queue for the dispatcher to process. Called by the Netty server handler on the Netty worker thread — returns immediately so the I/O thread is never blocked.
      Parameters:
      message - the message received from a connected component

    • takeInbound

      public Message<?> takeInbound() throws InterruptedException
      Blocking take from the inbound queue. Called exclusively by MessageDispatcherRunner on its dedicated thread.
      Returns:
      the next message to dispatch
      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if the dispatcher thread is interrupted

    • publish

      public void publish(Message<?> message, String serialised)
      Fans a message out to all channels currently subscribed to the message's topic.

      Called by MessageDispatcherRunner on its dedicated thread. For each subscriber of the topic, looks up their channel and writes the serialised message to it. If a subscriber's channel is no longer active, it is removed from the registry.

      Parameters:
      message - the message to fan out
      serialised - the message already serialised to a JSON string, ready to write

    • getInboundQueueDepth

      public int getInboundQueueDepth()
      Returns the number of messages currently waiting in the inbound queue. Useful for monitoring and diagnostics.
      Returns:
      inbound queue depth
